commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add location state caching for persistent settlements and dungeons

Implements US-OW-06: locations now cache their state (enemies, NPCs,
chests, traps, visibility, etc.) when the player leaves. Re-entering
a previously visited location restores the cached state instead of
regenerating. Cache persists through save/load (SAVE_VERSION 16).
Also fixes unreachable dead code bug in deserializeState where world
regeneration ran after an early return.

Fix new updates banner using IntersectionObserver for iframe scroll detection

The widget runs in an auto-resized iframe where window.scrollY is always 0.
Use IntersectionObserver on a sentinel element to detect when the user has
scrolled past the top of the feed in the parent page. Also fix scrollToTop
to use scrollIntoView which works across iframe boundaries.

B3 TE uniformity investigated: isotonic plateau confirmed, recalibration needed

TE dimension has 4 plateaus >0.5 raw units; largest spans 1.51 units (15%
of scale). Root cause: sparsity + raw isotonic (no quantile binning).
calibrate.py needs quantile-binned pre-aggregation across all dimensions.
TODO item added. Open question resolved.

commit.message:
Add MJPEG passthrough pipeline and zero-copy FrameStore to cut CPU/GC overhead

Phase 1: Try MJPEG passthrough (source ! image/jpeg ! fdsink) first,
falling back to decode+encode if device doesn't support it.
Phase 2: FrameStore uses ownership transfer instead of alloc+copy per frame.

before comparison. Normalize endpoint names (strip "from baseline").
Normalize route synonyms (IV→intravenous). Content-word overlap
fallback for endpoint name slot matching.

Endpoint name slot 44%→83%, timepoint 50%→67%, type 100%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: simplify Trainer — end-to-end training, LR warmup, dead code revival

- Remove staged VQ training (vq_start_epoch)
- Add LR warmup (LinearLR → CosineAnnealingLR)
- Add dead code revival every N epochs
- Log commit_loss and embed_loss separately
- Add gc.collect() + cuda.empty_cache() per epoch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: run コマンドで git worktree を使って作業を分離する (#101)

* feat: use git worktree in run command for isolated agent work

Agents like codex-cli can create their own branches during execution,
causing commits to land on the wrong branch. Using worktrees isolates
the agent's work directory so branch changes don't affect the main repo.

Closes #98

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use original repo path for worktree creation, not stale ctx.cwd

When resuming a run, ctx.cwd points to a previously deleted worktree.
Always use opts.cwd (the original repo path) for worktree operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: defer process.exit until after worktree cleanup

process.exit() terminates immediately, preventing the finally block
from cleaning up the worktree. Use an exitCode variable instead and
call process.exit after cleanup completes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: handle stale worktree from interrupted runs and add creation log

Remove any leftover worktree before creating a new one, preventing
'already exists' errors after Ctrl+C. Add logger.info on creation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: move worktree creation inside try block for structured error output

If addWorktree fails, the error is now caught and outputs structured
JSON to stdout instead of an unhandled exception trace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: skip worktree cleanup when creation failed

Only call removeWorktree in finally if addWorktree succeeded,
avoiding spurious "Worktree cleanup failed" error logs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix left/full join field metadata to mark optional fields, preventing nil panics

Left and full joins can produce nil values for fields from non-matching
datasources, but field metadata retained the original required=true flag.
Downstream consumers (NumericExpression, Condition, etc.) skip nil-wrapping
when Required()=true, causing nil.(float64) panics at runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(auth): server-side OIDC discovery to eliminate all CORS dependencies

The frontend was fetching /.well-known/openid-configuration directly
from Authentik, which is blocked by CORS. Now the backend performs
discovery server-side and returns the authorization_endpoint in the
/api/auth/oidc/config response. The frontend no longer makes any
cross-origin requests to the IdP.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(agent): Phase 9 — review, document, research & graph pipelines

Add remaining intent pipelines completing all 11 agent intents:

- Search provider: SearchProvider interface with BraveSearchProvider and
  ClaudeWebSearchProvider, factory auto-selects based on BRAVE_API_KEY
- Graph handler: zero-LLM structural queries via regex classification
  (callers/callees/depends/search/entity/cypher), re-routes interpretive
  questions to research pipeline
- Research pipeline: source selection heuristics (graph/web/combined),
  local model generates 1-3 search queries, deduplicates results,
  synthesizes with inline [N] citations, escalation on >2 repos or
  @claude prefix
- Review pipeline: context-assembly→Claude-review (NOT sketch→enhance),
  assembles diffs + entity bodies + 1-hop neighbour signatures,
  @opus escalation for deep architectural reviews
- Document pipeline: local generation as unified diff with doc type
  detection, existing comment style matching, opt-in --review flag,
  single-round Claude review, Sonnet escalation for cross-cutting
  docs (ADRs/changelogs)
- First-run Brave API key setup prompt (one-time, skippable)

112 tests passing (P9), all P1-P9 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- auth-service: normalize error messages to prevent info leakage; always return success on password reset to prevent email enumeration
- draft-outreach: extract logRun helper for agent_runs inserts (fire-and-forget, no added latency)
- Kanban Board: fix direct mutation of draft state; use immutable spread pattern

commit.message:
perf: fused SwiGLU+NVFP4 GEMV+residual kernel for decode

New gemv_nvfp4_swiglu_residual kernel computes SwiGLU activation
inline during the NVFP4 GEMV dot product, eliminating the separate
SwiGLU kernel launch and intermediate buffer write/read per layer.

Results (decode tok/s):
- Qwen3-4B: 273 → 288 (+5.5%)
- Qwen3-8B: 138 → 184 (+33%)
- GeGLU models (Gemma-3) use fallback path (separate activation)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add resumable chunked table copy with checkpoints and validation (#24)

* Add resumable chunked table copy with checkpoints and post-load validation

Tables with a single-column numeric PK are automatically split into
range-based chunks for parallel loading. Checkpoint state is persisted
after each chunk so interrupted migrations can resume without recopying
completed work. Optional row_count validation compares source and target
counts per table after the data phase.

New config fields: chunk_size (default 100000), resume (default false),
validation (default "none", supports "row_count").

Closes #21

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix integration test calls to use migrateDataConfig struct

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review feedback

- Fix concurrent map read/write: pre-compute skip sets from checkpoint
  before launching worker goroutines (P1)
- Warn when validation runs with single_tx mode on live sources (P1)
- Exclude unsigned bigint PKs from chunking to avoid int64 overflow (P2)
- Check srcRows.Err() after Next() returns false in validation
- Validate checkpoint version on load (reject unknown versions)
- Guard against int64 overflow in planChunks range calculation
- Add unit tests for validate.go, checkpoint version, unsigned bigint

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix finalizeConfig return signatures after rebase

fix: browser batch 支持 --file 模式避免 shell JSON 引号转义失败
